synthetic_media_rule: Disclosure required for realistic altered or synthetic content that could be mistaken for real people, places, scenes, or events.
creator_disclosure: Yes, for qualifying realistic altered or synthetic content.
automatic_labeling: Platform may apply labels in some cases; likeness detection exists for enrolled creators.
monetization_link: Potential penalties for missing disclosure; creator program implications depend on enforcement.

synthetic_media_rule: Labels AI generated content and manipulated media using creator disclosure and industry signals.
creator_disclosure: Yes, through AI info workflows and creator disclosure.
automatic_labeling: Uses industry signals including C2PA/IPTC where available.
monetization_link: Indirect; affects trust, reach, and policy enforcement more than direct public payout rules.

synthetic_media_rule: Requires labels for AI generated content that contains realistic images, audio, or video.
automatic_labeling: Uses Content Credentials for some automatic AIGC labels.
monetization_link: Creator Rewards emphasizes original, high quality content; policy violations can affect eligibility.

synthetic_media_rule: Prohibits deceptively shared synthetic or manipulated media likely to cause harm.
creator_disclosure: Context dependent; clear disclosure required for some AI conflict content under monetization policy.
automatic_labeling: May label posts containing synthetic and manipulated media.
monetization_link: Direct: undisclosed AI generated armed conflict videos can trigger Creator Revenue Sharing suspension.
